data.title: Sabre expands lodging content with agreement to add Premier Inn properties
data.description:
• Sabre Corporation (NASDAQ:SABR), on Thursday, announced a new distribution agreement with the UK’s hotel brand, Premier Inn, to ensure travel buyers worldwide have access to Premier Inn's expansive lodging content.
  • With over 900 properties and more than 85,000 rooms across the UK and Ireland, as well as 10,500 rooms in Germany, Premier Inn's properties are now available through Sabre's GDS.
  • This integration will enable travel agencies to book Premier Inn properties on the same platform as other travel components and services.
  • Sabre (SABR) shares were up more than 2% in pre-market trading on Thursday.

data.title: Allison Transmission announces expansion of its manufacturing facility in India
data.description:
Allison Transmission (NYSE:ALSN) announced on Thursday the expansion of its state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Chennai, India. The site is being used to support increasing global demand for Allison fully automatic transmissions. The company said the multi-year, over $100-million investment will double the existing manufacturing footprint of the Chennai facility.

In particular, the expansion will increase production capacity for Allison's (ALSN) on-highway fully automatic transmission portfolio, enhancing global operational flexibility and capability.

Allison Transmission (ALSN) said it continues to invest in its business to achieve its growth ambitions and deliver its brand promise to Improve the Way the World Works.

Shares of ALSN are up 8% over the last six weeks. The company is due to report earnings on October 29.

data.title: The Marygold Companies acquires 7.95% stake in Midland Capital
data.description:
• The Marygold Companies (NYSE:MGLD) said it has acquired a 7.95% ownership position, and invested $1.8 million, in Delaware based Midland Capital.
  • Midland Capital is a privately owned holding company.
  • As part of the deal, the firm also holds 180K warrants to purchase 180K additional shares of Midland Capital at an exercise price of $22.25.
  • The warrants are expected to expire on February 27, 2034.

data.title: Verizon, AT&T wireless upgrade rates show improvement, but still tepid: Wells Fargo
data.description:
Recent quarterly results from AT&T (NYSE:T) and Verizon (NYSE:VZ) showed improvements in wireless upgrades, but they're still coming at a "tepid" pace, Wells Fargo said.

AT&T added 403,000 postpaid phone net subscribers, with a churn rate of 0.93%. It also had an upgrade rate of 3.5%, up from 2.9% in the second quarter, but down from 3.9% in the year-ago quarter. Meanwhile, Verizon added 349,000 postpaid phone subscribers and had a churn rate of 1.16%. Verizon's upgrade rate during the quarter was 3%.

"On a combined basis, we would estimate AT&T + Verizon smartphone upgrades at -6.8M, -9% y/y compared to -17% y/y and -10% y/y in 1Q24 and 2Q24," analyst Aaron Rakers wrote in a note to clients.

"We see [Verizon and AT&T] results as reflective of an ongoing tepid smartphone upgrade environment," Rakers added, in reference to Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) iPhone lineup. Rakers has an Overweight rating and $275 price target on Apple.

On Wednesday, TF International Securities' analyst Ming-Chi Kuo said iPhone 16 orders have been cut by around 10M units for the next few quarters.

Apple is set to report fiscal fourth-quarter results after the close of trading on October 31. A consensus of analysts expect the company will earn $1.55 per share on $94.26B in revenue.

data.title: Nasdaq futures rise sharply on Tesla's robust earnings report
data.description:
Stock index futures were mixed on Thursday, with Nasdaq futures rising sharply as a strong earnings report from Tesla (TSLA) brought cheer to investors.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.5%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.9% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 4 basis points to 4.20%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 3 basis points to 4.06%.

Wall Street slumped on Wednesday, weighed down by a slide in technology and consumer discretionary stocks and a post-earnings plunge in some high-profile companies.

"Markets continued to slide down the wire yesterday, with bonds and equities both losing ground for a third day running. Maybe the long awaited close election sell-off is arriving, albeit after 6 successive weeks of rallying," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The U.S. sell-off yesterday was probably also influenced by investors dialing back the likelihood of aggressive rate cuts from the Federal Reserve, Reid added.

Tesla (TSLA) rose 10.8% in premarket trade after the firm broke a streak of missing EPS estimates with its last four quarterly reports and reiterated that it plans to make EVs affordable.

Market sentiment was also helped by some strong earnings report.

American Airlines (AAL) and Southwest Airlines (LUV) delivered a beat to both their top and bottom line estimates.

United Parcel Service (UPS) also climbed higher after it beat Q3 estimates.

The initial jobless claims unexpectedly dropped in the past week.

The September new home sales report is also due during market hours, and is expected to rise to 719K.

data.title: Chicago Fed National Activity Index turns negative in September
data.description:
• The Chicago Fed National Activity Index ("CFNAI") turned negative in September to -0.28 vs. -0.01 in August (revised from +0.12).
  • Two of the four broad categories of indicators used to construct the index decreased from August, and all four categories made negative contributions in September. The index's three-month moving average, CFNAI-MA3, decreased to –0.19 in September from –0.14 in August.
  • The CFNAI Diffusion Index, which is also a three-month moving average, increased to –0.16 in September from –0.21 in August. Thirty-eight of the 85 individual indicators made positive contributions to the CFNAI in September, while 47 made negative contributions. Thirty-nine indicators improved from August to September, while 43 indicators deteriorated and three were unchanged. Of the indicators that improved, 14 made negative contributions.
  • Production-related indicators contributed –0.21 to the CFNAI in September, down from +0.04 in August.
  • The sales, orders, and inventories category's contribution to the CFNAI was –0.03 in September, unchanged from August.
  • Employment-related indicators contributed –0.03 to the CFNAI in September, down from a neutral value in August.
  • The personal consumption and housing category's contribution to the CFNAI was –0.01 in September, up from –0.03 in August

data.title: Eramet surges after buying remaining stake in Argentina lithium project
data.description:
Eramet (OTCPK:ERMAF) (OTCPK:ERMAY) +8.2% in European trading Thursday after the French miner agreed to acquire full control of a lithium project in Argentina from Chinese partner Tsingshan Holding Group in a deal worth $699M.

Eramet (OTCPK:ERMAF) (OTCPK:ERMAY) said it wanted to retake full ownership to fully control the development of the resource, which currently has capacity to produce 24K tons/year of lithium carbonate equivalent, enough for 600K electric vehicles annually, with potential long-term production of more than 75K tons/year lithium-carbonate equivalent.

The Centenario project, due to start production in the coming weeks, was attractive despite a drop in lithium prices, and full ownership would let Eramet (OTCPK:ERMAF) (OTCPK:ERMAY)decide how to pursue a planned second production facility, Chair and CEO Christel Bories said.

The French government, which is one of Eramet's (OTCPK:ERMAF) (OTCPK:ERMAY) biggest shareholders, reacted positively to the deal, with Energy and Climate Minister Agnès Pannier-Runacher saying it "strengthens our critical raw materials sovereignty."

Tsingshan remains Eramet's (OTCPK:ERMAF) (OTCPK:ERMAY) partner in Indonesia, where they jointly operate a nickel mine.

data.title: Walgreens, Veeva Systems form partnership
data.description:
• Walgreens (NASDAQ:WBA) has struck a long-term partnership with Veeva Systems (NYSE:VEEV) to help life sciences companies improve patient outcomes.
  • The partnership enriches Veeva Data Cloud products and allows Walgreens (WBA) to better serve life sciences through access to Veeva Data Cloud and Veeva Clinical Platform.
  • "Partnering with Walgreens expands the breadth of data in Veeva Data Cloud, giving life sciences companies the high-quality data they need to better understand market trends and improve patient outcomes,” said Veeva CEO Peter Gassner.
